group.name: Base ROM
group.description: Before generating, you will need to provide a clean copy of the Wario's Woods (SNES) ROM binary image. Once you have done so, the rest of the form is enabled.

group.name: Generating
group.description: The main tab contains settings, a picture of Sarissa, and a button suggesting it can generate seeds.
Up to 100 seeds may be generated at a time, with said amount changeable by the box next to the generate button.
Files will be saved to the output directory as {{woodsrando-[hash] ([flags]).sfc}}.

group.name: Monsters
group.description: Choose which monster types will be randomized into each level.
group.item: Toggles every monster type
Toggle availability of Squeak
Toggle availability of Spud
Toggle availability of Fuzz
Toggle availability of Beaker
Toggle availability of Scram
Toggle availability of Dovo
Toggle availability of Spook

group.name: Play as
group.description: Choose which character graphics to use for the player
group.item: Play as this character.
Play as a random character

group.name: Board
group.description: Choose which algorithm to use for filling the board with monsters.
group.item: Boards are populated with a number of enemies and with column heights that scale with round number.
Boards are populated with few enemies and short columns.
Boards are populated with a fair number of enemies and reasonably tall columns.
Boards are populated with many enemies and tall columns.
Boards are populated with a lot of enemies and very tall columns.
Boards are populated by filling all columns to the same height of either 8 or 9 monsters tall.
Each board has its vanilla columns shuffled around.
Each board has the same number of monsters as in vanilla but with different columns.

group.name: Colors
group.description: Choose which algorithm to use for coloring monsters. Note that this only picks the maximum number; some colors may still end up unused just by chance.
group.item: The maximum number of colors per board scales with round number.
The maximum number of colors per board is 8.
The maximum number of colors per board is from 6 to 8.
The maximum number of colors per board is from 1 to 8.

group.name: Difficulty
group.description: Set the difficulty of the game, which controls Wario's aggressiveness, level pacing, and coins.
group.item: Difficulty scales with round number.
Slow-paced rounds. Coins scale with round number.
Medium-paced rounds. Coins scale with round number.
Fast-paced rounds. Coins do not scale with round number.
Very-fast-paced rounds. Coins do not scale with round number.
Every setting is all over the place with no correlation to be found anywhere.

group.name: RNG
group.description: Select an RNG algorithm to use for each seed.
group.item: Uses the vanilla RNG, which reads from a precalculated table of values.
Uses the vanilla RNG, but provides a new table of precalculated values.
Combines a simple LFSR with the vanilla table for the best of both worlds.
Uses a simple XORshift algorithm.
Uses the scanning-beam-based solution employed by Zelda 3.

group.name: Lives
group.description: Choose how many lives to start with when starting a new round game.
group.item: Start with 0 lives. This is the vanilla behavior.
Start with this many lives.
Start with 1 life that is never lost. No coins will be granted.

group.name: Theme
group.description: Choose the colors of the monsters and other items on the board.
Custom palettes can be defined by editing the included {{palettes.xml}} file. See Customization § Themes for more information.
group.item: Uses the vanilla theme.
Uses this theme.
Picks a random theme from the available list.
Generates a new theme from random palettes of appropriate colors.
Generates a new theme from random palettes.
Generates a random theme using the same hues as vanilla.

group.name: Song
group.description: Choose the song to play during round game.
group.item: Play this song
Plays the egg crack sound to prevent a song from playing.
Chooses a random song.

group.name: Other options
group.item: In endurance mode, your progress is remembered, but you cannot continue from the last checkpoint. You must always restart from round 1 after a game over.
When simple rounds are enabled, each round will only have 1 level associated with it. This is in contrast to vanilla, where most rounds have 2 levels.
With direct deposit, all coins earned at the end of a round are automatically added to your total, allowing for speedier games without sacrificing safety.
Initialize the control scheme at generation time.

group.name: Themes
group.description: Included with the app is the file {{palettes.xml}}. This file contains 3 groups of items to customize the board palette.
Detailed instructions on the format are contained in the file at the top.
group.item: This node contains individual {{<color>}} nodes with a case-insensitive name and a color-shade pair for easy reuse.
This node contains individual {{<palette>}} nodes with a case-insensitive name and the definitions for 7 colors.
This node contains individual {{<palette>}} nodes with a case-insensitive name and the definitions for 7 colors.

group.name: Skins
group.description: This app creates a file named {{sprites.xml}} after you provide it with a vanilla ROM.
Files for custom player skins are kept in the {{data}} folder. These files should be left alone.
